reviews.review_description: When I first tried this game I didn’t like it much and felt it was very hard.. didn’t play it again for months.. then I attended my first MotoGP race a few week ago in Austin TX.. That got me excited about riding again., I decided to try this game again and work hard to learn the proper technique of motorcycle racing (leaning, throttle control, break control, body position).,  I hold my arms out with elbows close to body and lean to take corners so it’s pretty realistic that the way.. I am now loving this game and have improved massively!! It is very realistic and a ton of fun!! It is also improving my real life riding.. please add Red Bull Ring and COTA😁😁

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the amazing feedback, and glad you've been enjoying your experience in VRider SBK! :D

We will surely send your suggestions to our team. See you in the tracks!

reviews.review_description:
I got me the 2 circuits Estoril and Cremona but when i start the game it wont instal them due to an error. It says continue or retry. No matter how often i try to restaet install is getting stuck trying to instal the 2 circuits.
Can you help me out plz? 
Regards Jean

Deleting cache first and uninstall -install the app again worked 
I can race now in the dlc circuits.
I chaned to 5 stars because of the active dev and help from him. Also because its a cool motor racegame.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! <3

reviews.review_description: I’m a huge fan of VR racing games and this happens to be a very good example. One of the key things that this game brings is adding a decent amount of skill in cornering. This makes winning a more satisfying experience due to the new and unique handling system involving raising/lowering either controller to achieve the correct lean angle. There are a handful of good tracks and more via dlc. This is a very slick experience and multiplayer is a blast. Great work devs! 🛞🏍️🔥

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for the review and feedback! :D

reviews.review_description: The racing mechanics are actually pretty fun, but go into this knowing you’ll be riding alone. While there is a multiplayer mode, the servers are a total ghost town and the community Discord is silent. If you’re looking for competition against real people, you won’t find it here. It’s a decent enough experience for a quick solo fix, but between the lack of players and some persistent graphical glitches, the novelty wears off pretty fast. There are also no stats for the bikes and only 1 class you can race with.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback. Please join our Discord if you wish to race other players! We have a very active community over there :)

Every piece of feedback is very appreciated! 

https://discord.gg/sv7D3E7HSE

reviews.review_description:
...... Like a classic movie on its first watch .. ya not sure what all the fuss is about .. but the more u watch (play) the more u understand & appreciate ..next thing u know your watching (playing 😜) every opportunity u get .. 

Lean to steer would make this almost perfect 

Cheers 

Carl 😊

developer_response.body:
Thank you very much for the review, Carl!

We will send your feedback to the team. See you in the tracks. :D

reviews.review_description: It gets boring quickly. Let's get a neighborhood road with some traffic and do some street races! 🔥🏁 Weaving pedestrian vehicles trying to beat your opponent to the finish line could be a nice touch!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback. We’re glad you enjoyed the game. We appreciate your suggestions around adding more dynamic environments and race variety, and we’ll definitely take them into consideration as we continue to improve the experience.

Please take a moment to join our Discord community and get in touch with the team there: https://discord.gg/sv7D3E7HSE

reviews.review_description: The game freezes after the start screen. All I can see are the flying cubes. I've had the game since it first came out. Today, for the first time in a long time, there was an update, which didn't finish. Since then, it's been over.

Hello, thank you for bringing this to our attention. We are sorry to hear that the recent update did not install correctly and is causing the game to freeze.

If you continue to experience problems, please contact our support team directly so we can investigate this further for you. Thank you for your patience and for being a long-time player.

Please take a moment to join our Discord community and get in touch with the team there: https://discord.gg/sv7D3E7HSE

reviews.review_title: Good, but could be a whole lot better
reviews.review_description: Right, I’ve always wanted a MotoGP vr game. I was hoping this would be it, sadly as good as it is, it still leaves a lot to be desired. My main gripe is even after the updates you still get frame drops during a race. Now it’s not as bad as it was in first release, but they’re still present. Game looks are okay, bike is good but trackside not as good, and probably very much a limitation of the hardware. The biggest disappointment is the lack of chest steering. V racer has it and it’s really well done, and if it could be implemented here would make the experience a whole lot better. It’s a good game, but as the title says, could be so much better

developer_response.body:
Hello Maverick118, thank you so much for the review and your suggestions! We'll forward them to our team, and discuss internally. 🙌

Happy racing!

reviews.review_description: Let me get a refund. horrible game steering was trash!! I need a REFUND NOW GIVE ME A REFUND REFUND REFUND REFUND

After VRacer Hoverbike just released there's no reason to not implement chest steering in all motorcycle sims in VR. Moving two unconnected controllers and not having immersive leaning makes the experience disconnected. After VRacer I couldn't find enjoyment in driving this even though it is awesome in so many ways. Please have the devs spend sometime with the control and camera in VRacer and implement this to put this game over the top.

developer_response.body:
Hey there!

Thank you so much for the feedback. We'll let our team know about your experience, especially related to handling the bike. 🫡

reviews.review_description: Maybe i am doing something wrong, but when i am steering it feels like bike slides in oposite direction under me, not drifting, just repositions itself so handlebars doesnt seem to be where my hands are. It doesnt matter if i lean my body or not. Any tips?

developer_response.body:
Hey Iaada, you need to create a vertical gap between the two controllers; the vertical distance corresponds to the lean of the bike. I recommend lowering the shoulder on the inside of the curve, then bending your upper body and looking toward the inside of the curve with your head. Ideally, you could connect the two controllers with a bar.
The controller's vertical distance indeed steers the bike, but if you also use your body, you can ride for hours without motion sickness. There’s no need to keep your arms raised; you can bring your elbows closer to your body with less effort, and some people even ride with their elbows resting on their legs.
You should see the motorbike slightly from the side because when cornering, the rider leaves the symmetry plane of the bike.
Thank you for the stars! Every good rating gives us a better chance to continue developing VRider SBK.

reviews.review_description: Has potential to be a great tool for real riders to practice certain aspects of riding, the graphics are good, physics okay and controls are intuitive. EXCEPT for the steering. While it makes sense in theory, coming from real world riding experience it confuses the heck out of me when counter steering is no longer a thing. Please please please add the option to either invert the controls for steering or just a “counter steer” option for real life riders.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review, we'll make sure to forward your feedback regarding counter steering to our developers. ❤️

reviews.review_description: Took me about 3 races to get the hang of it and now im on it every night going for my fastest lap times. You can race against bots or online lobby's or race peoples ghost times to get your ranking up for that track. Twist throttle to go or choose trigger, left trigger for break and lean hands/body to turn and tuck your head in to get out of the wind makes for a really good experience.

developer_response.body: We are extremely happy to hear about your positive experience with VRider! Hope to see you on the track one of these days!! 🛵🏆

reviews.review_description:
Really cool experience ever after I felt like my dad because he was a a world champion of motorcycle racing ￼
￼

developer_response.body: You are a champ as well now! Thank you! ❤️

reviews.review_title: I like the game but add willie's , pleas
reviews.review_description: I really do like the game, but can you guys really just please add Willie's to the game?It'll make it wait for under end like a clutch button like I'm b you like, whatever please.I'll start playing like the game way more

developer_response.body: Wheelies, you say.. Perhaps that update is closer than you think! 👀

reviews.review_description:
As a rider of 30 years or so, I was excited to jump on a liter bike in VR. 
VRider delivers. 
It delivers a good sensation of speed and control. I can run hot laps until my battery warning starts popping up. It very much reminds me of the MotoGP games I played years ago. Hitting a new personal best lap time is so satisfying. Blow a corner and you will feel the frustration as your ghost zipps past. 🤣
Track knowledge is key. I hot lap a track several times before dropping into a race.

I did struggle with the controls in the beginning. Just suspending your hands in the air did not give a positive sense of control. I found a solution in a very unlikely place. A cheap plastic coat hanger. Place your wrists inside the coat hanger and grab the controllers. This forces the hands into a single plane. My lap time immediately improved, and it was a more immersive experience. I plan on 3d printing a less "junkyard" solution, but this works well in the meantime. I just ignore the weird looks and chuckles from the wife...  😂

developer_response.body:
We're glad you found a solution! Any method to bind the controllers (even using a piece of a broom) helps you achieve a more natural posture and riding experience, making you lean into curves with your shoulders. The sensation is truly unique!

If you join our VRider SBK Discord, you can also connect with other players and organize multiplayer sessions.

Enjoy VRider SBK.

reviews.review_description:
The game's look, feel, it's mechanics- just everything seems great (even if I would've needed A LOT more time to fully adjust to controlling the bike well enough to win anything ever... 😅. Not an issue though), but the game after the initial" wow" factor is gone, leaves absolutely nothing for a solo player who has no interest whatsoever in multiplayer or score chasing. No campaign of any kind, no unlockables, nothing to work towards/ for besides beating scores and completing time set times for no other reason than doing it.
So whilst the mechanics and the absolutely glorious speed are excellent, I would have zero incentive to play after maybe an hour in total, especially with my very limited (zero peripheral) vision making an already tough game/ sim even more difficult. 

So it's not for me, but for those who enjoy score chasing, multiplayer, and/ or are crazy about motor bike racing- it's definitely worth getting.
If like me (solo only, no care for scores, need incentives to keep playing with campaigns-unlocks-etc...), I would avoid this game as after a few races, there's nothing really left and you likely just spent $30 for a title you'll rarely come back to unless your just that crazy about the rush of speed.

developer_response.body:
Hi Teele891,
Thanks for your review!
The game challenges you to become the Champion by competing across 14 unique tracks in the World Championship in Single Player.
Of course, we believe that both synchronous and asynchronous multiplayer challenges can enhance the experience and keep you engaged for longer.
With enough reviews and not 2-star ratings, we can continue improving the game, and a career mode is definitely on the table for solo players!
Hope you keep enjoying VRider SBK!

reviews.review_description: Updated review after some time. The developers have addressed a lot of my initial concerns and issues and have deployed many improvements in several regards. However, it’s still a 3 star product for me personally. Frame drops around the tracks are still a major issue. There’s very little progression or reasons to keep you playing. Online is completely dead and maybe I’ll occasionally find one other person online, but I’ve never once had a competitive race. The devs are clearly talented, but the overall direction of the game seems to be holding it back. It needs to be much more fleshed out and polished to be considered a worthwhile racing game. A career based progression system is desperately needed

developer_response.body:
Thanks for updating your review and for the thoughtful feedback.

We’re really glad you’ve noticed the improvements so far. We hear you on performance, progression, and online activity. These are all key areas we’re actively working on. Optimization is ongoing, and we agree that a stronger progression system, including career-style features, is important for long-term engagement.

We truly appreciate your honest input. It helps guide where we take the game next. :D

reviews.review_description: Took a punt on this one but like others have said, it loses appeal very quickly and becomes boring, the graphics are OK on the bike, average for the track but the sound is terrible. Fun for about 10 mins

developer_response.body:
Thanks for giving it a try and for sharing your honest feedback. We really appreciate it!

We’re glad you enjoyed the bike visuals, even if only briefly, and we’re sorry to hear the experience didn’t hold your interest. Pacing and long-term engagement are things we’re actively working to improve, and your comment about it becoming repetitive is especially helpful for us!

Thanks again for taking the time to share your thoughts. It genuinely helps us make the experience better.

reviews.review_description: Let me start by saying that I'm not very good at racing games.but I still enjoy this game. I will say there's a little bit of a learning curve but not too bad. Depending on the time of day you'll be able to find a match online if that's your thing. If you want to play by yourself, the game's AI provides a good challenge. If you're looking for the closest thing to a motorcycle simulator, I'm guessing this is it. But then again I'm not an expert when it comes to those kind of games. If you want something where you can hop in for 5 to 10 minutes with a quick race, this is the game.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for the glowing review! Glad you are enjoying your time with the game! :D

reviews.review_description:
I honestly was worried with dropping money to this because every game for motorcycles always fall short, but this game!!!! I'm blown away.... Now do motocross fans a favor and make a motocross/super 
s since y'all got body physics down 😉

But after playing some, I'm having real issues finding filled lobbies, unless there's only ever 2 people playing  in total for multi-player, I never get to race with anyone, idk if it's my system or what

Only downside is not enough people play on multiplayer and I'm having a hard time finding online races. Please get this game, you won't regret it. And please play online

reviews.review_title: Why not add a counter steering option
reviews.review_description: I asked for a counter steering option, but it's not needed. This game is a must have

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for the feedback, and hope you are having fun in the tracks!

reviews.review_description: I love this game. I’m an advid fan of motogp, sbk etc. I just wish more people played this, but definitely give it a shot

developer_response.body: Hey Dirtybirds8, On the VRider SBK Discord, players organize multiplayer sessions, and we also host special events from time to time. Join us and have fun with VRider SBK!

reviews.review_description:
First off, thanks for making and developing a game like this. Keep going. 

This is a fun game I bought after reading the reviews. 
BUT, just to add some balance to the reviews, this is not close to the real thing at all. Perhaps I have the wrong settings on, but my main gripe is the steering.
You don't turn a real bike by lifting up a handle bar , you countersteer and shift weight which I understand would make the game extremely difficult to get right without real world inertia and gyroscopic effects to resist your movements but what bothers me is when you do turn you can change direction just as quickly as 10mph as 190mph which those forces just don't allow in reality but thats also what makes real bikes stable. 
Honestly, I think they need to film someone playing the game and think to themselves, "Do those movements mimic real riding?" Because I can tell you now they won't.

Better yet, get an sbk rider to sit on a bike and do a mental lap of a track with the bike hooked up to sensors then try to setup the steering motion input to the game for a more realistic experience.

(For reference, I've raced on some of the game tracks in real life, so I've got a good idea of how it should feel)

developer_response.body: Hey Blinkered, thanks a lot for your honest review. As your review mentions, we did not make a true-to-life simulation game, it's a Simcade title meaning it has Simulation and Arcade elements. We have attended multiple World SBK events and have had actual SBK riders play our game (look on our socials). They were very impressed with the experience and were able to beat our in-house lap times in less than 15 mins! Adding countersteer into the equation was something we tested a lot, but ultimately decided to go with the tried and true method of our current steering. If you feel the bike moves too fast when steering, try reducing the steering sensitivity to your liking.

reviews.review_description:
Nicely done, feels pretty close to the real thing 
Would be absolutely mint if we could get some street maps with bikes like the SV650, GSXR 750, MT07, B12, some other hooligan bikes
Or even mini twin racing, superbikes are cool and all… but some night time street racing would be siiiiiiick, you already have the system

developer_response.body: Dear Drako, we are thrilled to hear and read your positive feedback with VRider. Since the SBK is a licensed brand we are limited to what is present in the official World SBK season. Therefore we cannot add bikes, brands or tracks that aren't present in the championship. This doesn't mean that aren't going to take advantage of our tech 😉 Stay tuned for more updates in the future!

reviews.review_description: Game should be called "please wait."  Have to wait a super  long time for offline option. Played it but it takes too long to wait.   It was a mistake to give this game a second chance.  Now no refund. I'll play the delete function.

developer_response.body: Hey there, if you are facing connectivity issues we recommend you close the game, restart your hardware, and ensure you have an active connection when playing. Ensuring your internet speed is key for the game to load between modes. We also have an offline option if you keep facing this issue!

reviews.review_description: The pivot point in this game is all wrong.  If you play VRacer and MotoX they get this perfect this game does not.  As everyone plays this game sitting in a seat, the seat becomes the pivot point the bike should rotate through.  Developers, you need to play VRacer to understand this and set the screen tilt 60%. You'll fully understand this in 5 seconds of playing this game. Please fix this, in real life the pivot point is the ground as thats where the bike rotates through in VR it is always the seat the VR player is sitting on.

developer_response.body:
Hi mnc808, in VRider SBK there’s no need to press the grip buttons, just like on a real motorcycle, you already have the handlebars in your hands by simply holding the controllers. This design makes the controls simpler and more realistic, ensuring you won’t “lose” the handlebars or the bike by forgetting to press a button (after all, you don’t drive a real motorcycle by pressing a button!).

If you’re losing control of the rear, you can activate traction control as a driving aid, just like on real motorcycles.

Please note that refunds are managed by the Store and governed by Meta’s policies, not the game itself.

reviews.review_description: I would have given a 5 star if we could please do some wheelies!! You guys have potential! Let's see a good street bike game!

developer_response.body: We promise to add the wheelie in one of the upcoming releases, so stay tuned!

reviews.review_description:
This game is one of the main reasons I picked up a quest. And it did not disappoint. I will be putting a lot of time into this.

I did buy the 2 add on tracks, but they will not install. Not sure what to do. I have to hit cancel on the startup screen to advance to game. But they won't install.

Tried the suggestions but still no go. Un-installed and reinstalled game, same thing. Every once and awhile while the green circle is going around i see a box pop up that says confirm your purchase, but disappears instantly.

Ok so the tracks i were missing just showed up today lol.

developer_response.body: Hey there Jeff - thank you for your positive feedback. We are sorry to hear about your issues with the new tracks. If you have completed the In App Purchase, closing the game, re-opening and clicking to play the new circuits should force the app to update and allow you to play. If the button to play still says 'buy' please try clicking on it and it should force the app to reload its data with the server and grant you access to play.

reviews.review_description: As the owner of a relatively tame real bike (gsxs750), this simulator is to me, the next best thing to an upgrade to a 1ltre race bike...Les the expense and crashes of the real thing.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for the glowing review! Glad you are enjoying your time with the game! :D

reviews.review_description: I have rode my entire life, dirt and sport mostly, and I must say that this game is amazing! Treat yourself to the best vr motorcycle racing on quest  😏☝️. Great job developers 🤜🤛 ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

developer_response.body: Hey PixelparadiceVr! Thank you so much for your positive review. It means a lot to know you enjoy our game!! See you on the race track

reviews.review_description: The game makes you pick a rider at the start and then you're stuck with it. All the top scores on the leaderboards have better bikes and I'm stuck with the one I picked. Can't even request a refund because I've had the game for more than 2 weeks and just now realizing I can't change my bike. For this reason the game gets 1 star.

developer_response.body:
Hi D3moniC1oud, in the central panel of the three visible when you launch a game mode (e.g., Quick Race), you’ll find the "rider" field listed usually in the second or third position. Use the right and left arrows to change the rider, and the bike will update in real-time in the main menu.

We’d appreciate it if you could reconsider your review, as a 1-star rating doesn’t help us improve the game. Thanks for your support!

reviews.review_description: This is the best racing game to date.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for the glowing review! Glad you are enjoying your time with the game! :D

reviews.review_description: Cool game, great concept! But multiplayer is dead!!!

developer_response.body: Hey there, we're sorry to hear that you could not find any MP races. We have an active community on Discord where we let our fans know when a racing lobby is up so we can all race together! We would love to have you join us and host or join races with our VRider fans!

reviews.review_description:
I primarily use my quest 3 for pcvr sim racing using iracing and assetto corsa.  I have found vriders to be a solid standalone option to scratch the racing itch without having to bother with pcvr.  The graphics, sound, and controls are all solid for standalone.

I am also an ex club motorcycle racer and have a lot of time on track and in the canyons irl.  This game is more of an arcade experience and does not use proper push pull counterstearing.  But it doesn't take long to get used the raising and lowering the controls to induce lean.  I do like that you can induce more lean angle by getting your head out near where the mirrors would be, as you would irl.  There does not appear to be separation of front and rear braking which is a bit of a bummer.  

Overall, the experience is fun and satisfying, though more arcade than sim if that matters to you.
I'll keep this short... awesome game... all we need now is a clutch option for wheelies and replay mode!... thanks
No players verry basic not worth £20 and doesn't let you get a refund verry angry

developer_response.body: Hi there, we are sorry to hear about your experience. Usually cross any Meta Quest game, if you play for more than 2 hours then you cannot refund the game. This has to do with Meta services and not with our game specifically <3

reviews.review_description:
I did not enjoy this game nearly as much as I hoped I would have. If you like forza Motorsport you’d probably like this…I do not like forza…so yeah…👍

Also for the love of god meta add my driving games, not racing games like this or grid, stuff like mud runner… that’s not mud runner why do I go half a mile in 90% of the time in game.
Anyway yeah I didn’t enjoy this game😐

developer_response.body: Hi there, we are sorry you didn't like VRider SBK. This game is for those who enjoy high speeds, split-second decisions and competitive online multiplayer races. If you do not like any of these genres, then maybe VRider isn't a 100% match for you <3

reviews.review_description:
b i t , l y / v r i d e r s b k

Great motorcycle game. Exactly what I was hoping for!
So many features, god it’s such an amazing game!!

reviews.review_title: One of the best VR Games.
reviews.review_description: Let's start with the tutorial. It was perfect. It taught me everything about the game and the controls so I could even race. I love how it feels way better than any other racing game on any device. My favorite parts of the game would be the turning controls and the ghost racing in multiplayer. My least favorite parts of the game are how there are never any online servers (at least not when I was playing) and there is no customization to the very few bikes the game has.

developer_response.body:
Hi skittlesallday18, thank you very much for the kind words, we really appreciate it!

As far as MP availability, we are still in early stages and we're building up a healthy online community too (I'd advise you to visit our website and join our discord so that it can be easier to set play dates with other players).

Regarding bike customisation, we are unfortunately limited to the bikes and riders that are available in the official World Superbike Championship, however we are working with the license holder to allow us some more wiggle room when it comes down to this sort of stuff in future updates!

reviews.review_description: The graphics are really nice for VR, controls are intuitive gestures for VR but have available arcade styles if you prefer. Though I wish they had a non VR input for the turning to try, as most racing games in VR I've played required a gaming controller. The ducking down to tuck and get speed also I wish had a button input option as my neck/back got tired quickly from ducking my head and holding. Finally getting a hang of the mechanics in VR didn't take long and once I was hitting turns perfect and passing racers I really loved the racing. Wish there was more challenges to perform to hone in my skills in something other than a race. Overall a very fun game with room to grow.

I bought this Game yesterday and I have to say that it's AMAZING!!! It feels so realistic! At the beginning it takes you a while to get used to the leaning but after couple races it gets better. Personally I would love to see arms or gloves or even a body. I can recommend this to all Bikers on rainy days.
A real bike steers by countersteering, please add option to inverse controls so that when you pull right handle the bike leans left. THAT WOILD MAKE IT A 5 STARRR

This game has so much going for it at the time of writing this. Graphics, sounds, track design all come together for a pretty cohesive MotoGP experience. There’s a pretty fleshed out tutorial to teach you more than just the basics. It gets into turning, braking, accelerating and a combination of the three.

There are some noticeable features missing right now, namely multiplayer. But what is there already is awesome. Especially if you’re not prone to motion sickness. There is a built-in MotionWellness setting that is on by default (thankfully) but even still, motion sickness would start to sink in between the 3rd and 5th laps (endurance) of a race, depending on the race track.

If you’re a fan of MotoGP or have always wanted to experience super bike speed and handling without the IRL dangers definitely give this a go!
If you're looking for a good racing game on Quest, this is for you. Bike handling is super immersive and fun.

reviews.review_title: Amazing Racing game - none like it
reviews.review_description: If you are looking for a VR Sim racing game, VRider is the best in the market. Amazing graphics and gameplay, feels very rewarding to come in first place. A must buy 100% for any racing or bike enthusiast!
Very good bike game. Congratulations to the devs. (I play on quest3 with qgo profile)

The thing that struck me with this one was the feeling of speed, which feels way too real. You can turn with your body and chose to use your hands or the triggers to speed up or down, which make it very easy to find the best way to drive. I’m using a quest 3 and the graphics are superb although I did notice a weird white shine in the demo bike, not sure what that was about. Other than that, the game is solid, looking forward to playing some more :D

Extremely imersive gameplay. Great game to get into vr racing. Would 100% recommend getting it.

Considering that the game is still working out features and other things it is great! The bots are extremely hard to beat, even on easy i ended up in last place pretty quickly. The tutorial gives everything you or need to know and is actually a challenge if you know nothing like me. Hope this helps! 
P.S. (The bike can turn a lot more than you think!)
